instructions.instruction: Use this skill when the task is to review or improve the structure of a Rails application or library.
instructions.original_snippets: Use this skill when the task is to review or improve the structure of a Rails application or library.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger review-architecture.

instructions.instruction: **Core principle:** Prioritize boundary problems over style. Prefer simple objects and explicit flow over hidden behavior.
instructions.original_snippets: **Core principle:** Prioritize boundary problems over style. Prefer simple objects and explicit flow over hidden behavior.

instructions.instruction: Check whether abstractions clarify the design or only move code around.
instructions.original_snippets: Check whether abstractions clarify the design or only move code around.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger review-architecture.

instructions.instruction: **Verify each High-severity finding** by reading the actual code — confirm it is a real structural problem, not just a pattern match on file size or line count. If verification reveals the finding is not a genuine structural problem, either downgrade it to Medium/Low with a revised rationale, or remove it entirely. Do not list findings that do not survive code-level confirmation.
